name: Andouille in Comforting Barbecue Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the peanut oil in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Add the onions and cook until they are clear or tender, about 10 minutes.
3. Add the celery, bell pepper, and parsley, and continue cooking for another 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
5. Pour in the steak sauce, hot sauce, and ketchup. Season with salt to taste.
6. Stir in the Southern Comfort and bring the mixture to a boil.
7. Reduce the heat to low and simmer uncovered for 2 to 3 hours, stirring occasionally.
8. Slice 1 lb of Andouille sausage into 1/4 inch thick pieces.
9. Combine the sliced sausage with 1 cup of the prepared sauce.
10. Heat the remaining sauce in a skillet over medium heat until warmed through, or reheat in a chafing dish.
11. Serve hot on slices of French bread or use toothpicks to spear and serve individual portions. Use plenty of napkins.
This will keep in the refrigerator for several weeks. Other smoked sausages may be substituted if desired.
